concept: Rough / Having Uneven Surface / Not Smooth
description: Having an uneven or irregular surface; not smooth or level. 'Keloa' names the quality of roughness—texture that catches, surface with variation.
sound_symbolism: The word has texture. 'Ke' begins with slight catch, surface encountered. 'Lo' moves across unevenly, variation felt. Finally 'a' opens to the full texture, roughness known.
grammatical_notes: Functions as an adjective: 'keloa salu' (rough smonae). Opposite of 'helu' (smooth). Can describe physical texture or metaphorical difficulty.

pillars.art-nouveau-aesthetics: Rough textures added interest—contrast between smooth and rough created visual depth.
pillars.pre-industrial-wisdom: Rough surfaces had purpose—grip for tools, traction for paths, texture for holding.
